title: UnifaceEntity
breadcrumbs: APIs: Java Call-In Classes and Methods
text: UnifaceEntity This constructor is used to create an instance for an entity with specific types of fields. Explain this code import com.rocketsoftware.uniface.jci.*; UnifaceEntity (int[] fieldTypes) Copy Parameters fieldTypes —an array containing the types of each of the fields required. The array is numbered starting with zero, which is an exception to the general Uniface convention. Table 1. Field Type Constants Constant Meaning UTYPE_STRING Uniface string UTYPE_BOOLEAN Uniface boolean UTYPE_NUMERIC Uniface numeric UTYPE_FLOAT Uniface float UTYPE_DATE Uniface date UTYPE_TIME Uniface time UTYPE_DATETIME Uniface date time UTYPE_RAW Uniface raw UTYPE_IMAGE Uniface image Return Values Returns an empty entity, that is, with no occurrences. Description Uniface entities are usually returned through parameters in Uniface operations, but they can also be created from Java using this class. Use the UnifaceEntity class to: Get entity information, such as the number of fields and the current number of occurrences Create new occurrences Remove occurrences Get data from and set data in fields of a specific occurrence The UnifaceEntity class is used to specify entity parameters from Java. The Java UnifaceEntity object is passed to Uniface as a Java object ( IN ), and from Uniface to Java as a Java object ( OUT ). The Java UnifaceEntity object is a local copy only. Note: Occurrences are passed by value, not by reference. When entity data is passed as an IN (or INOUT ) parameter, all occurrences are considered new data. A subsequent store from ProcScript, therefore, most likely leads to duplicate key errors. Methods UnifaceEntity.createOccurrence UnifaceEntity.getfieldType UnifaceEntity.getNumberOfFields UnifaceEntity.getNumberOfOccurrences UnifaceEntity.getOccurrenceField UnifaceEntity.removeOccurrence UnifaceEntity.removeOccurrences UnifaceEntity.setOccurrenceField UnifaceEntity.toString To specify an entity named UE, with two fields, the first field of type String and the second of type Numeric, you would use the following code: Explain this code int fieldTypes [] = new int [2]; fieldTypes[0] = Values.UTYPE_STRING; fieldTypes[1] = Values.UTYPE_NUMERIC; UnifaceEntity UE = new UnifaceEntity(fieldTypes); Copy Note: The fieldTypes array is numbered starting from zero. This is an exception to the general Uniface convention. In addition, non-database fields are not transported through the URB interface—if you need to access an entity that contains non-database fields, you should omit them from the fieldTypes array.
